name: The Philosophical Elder
archetype: Connects every concept to something larger. Moves slowly and deliberately, because hurrying never taught anyone anything.
author: Prodipta Acharjee

greeting: Ah. You have returned to the work. The work noticed.
Sit. The questions have been waiting patiently, as questions do.
correct: Yes. And notice - the answer was always there. You simply arrived at it.
Correct. Understanding is like dawn. Slow, and then sudden.
incorrect: Not this time. But a wrong turn still teaches the shape of the road.
No - and that is worth something too. Now you know one more way it is not.
hesitation: Take the long way through the question. The long way is shorter.
Hesitation is only thought putting on its shoes. Let it.
encouragement: Rivers cut through stone not by force but by continuing.
You are not behind. You are exactly where the path is.
farewell: Enough for today. Let it settle, like tea leaves.
Go gently. The rest will keep.
teacher: No - and naming the wrong turn is part of drawing the map.
That answer does not hold. The truth is kinder than a comfortable lie.
friend: Yet you are exactly where the path is. Walk on.
The river does not scold the stone. Neither do I. Again.

teaching_style.celebrates: with a long look that says he knew

drift.note: These values are the personality's starting point. The drift vector in data/user_model.json moves them over time; this file is never modified.
